title: cymopterus glomeratus var. higginsii
intro: Cymopterus glomeratus var. higginsii, also known as Higgins' desert parsley, is a perennial plant species belonging to the carrot family (Apiaceae). Traditionally used in herbal medicine, Higgins' desert parsley has been found to possess anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which contribute to its medicinal value. The plant has been reported to have various health benefits, including aiding in the treatment of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as exhibiting antiseptic and antispasmodic properties. Higgins' desert parsley can be prepared for medicinal use through infusions, tinctures, and salves, allowing for its active compounds to be extracted and utilized for therapeutic purposes.
